app.name: AI Star Composer
app.tagline: Orbital motion from NASA JPL Horizons, turned into music.

notFound.title: Lost in space
notFound.body: The page you are looking for has slipped beyond the heliopause. Try one of the inner orbits.

offline.banner: You are offline — showing cached data only. Reconnect to generate fresh results.

welcome.headline: Listen to the planets
welcome.sub: Choose a world, pull live ephemeris data, and hear it as melody, harmony, and rhythm — baseline physics or AI-scored transitions.
welcome.ctaSkipHint: No account required. Built for demos and thesis defense.

intro.aria: Cinematic intro to AI Star Composer
intro.kicker: AI Star Composer · powered by NASA Horizons
intro.title: Tune into the universe
intro.sub: Eight worlds, real ephemeris, your song.

features.realDataBody: Live JPL ephemeris drives every note. The same window can be cached for 6h to keep generation snappy.
features.aiTitle: AI vs baseline, side by side
features.aiBody: Compare a transition-scored AI mode against the direct physics baseline on identical inputs and the same seed.
features.demoBody: Demo mode walks through three planets, auto-plays each clip, and renders an interactive piano roll for every result.

studio.title: Studio
studio.subtitle: Generate MIDI/WAV from Horizons data, or compare baseline vs AI on the same window.

capabilities.title: Render pipeline
capabilities.lstmLine: LSTM checkpoint file: {{ready}} (set LSTM_CHECKPOINT_PATH or pass path in advanced)

planetPicker.hint: NASA Horizons · drag / tap planet / arrows · double-click for orbit
planetPicker.moonMission: Earth-to-Moon mission path active (rocket + Moon shown).

form.title: Parameters
form.nasaBgHint: Place .wav files in assets/backgrounds/ on the server.
form.useLstm: Blend trained LSTM into AI melody
form.planetHint: Pick a world in the fullscreen 3D view (left / top).
form.lstmTemperatureHint: Lower = stricter to training distribution. Higher = more exploratory.

results.emptyGenerate: Run Generate to see artifacts and metrics here.

live.title: Live stream
live.subtitle: WebSocket note events with Web Audio playback — the same engine that powers the Studio Demo tour.
live.noTelemetry: Start the stream to see live telemetry.
live.audioHint: Tip: if you hear silence, click Start again — browsers require a user gesture to unlock AudioContext.
live.audioBlocked: Could not start audio (browser blocked or unavailable).
live.socketError: WebSocket error — is uvicorn running on port 8000?
live.useLstmBaselineHint: Switch to AI mode to enable LSTM blending on the stream.
live.useLstmEnvHint: Server must have a valid checkpoint (LSTM_CHECKPOINT_PATH) and torch installed.

quality.title: Quality dashboard
quality.subtitle: Wilcoxon signed-rank tests comparing AI mode to the baseline on paired generations. Significant rows (p < α) are highlighted.
quality.runHint: Capped at {{max}} pairs to stay under one HTTP request. Use scripts/evaluate_baseline_vs_ai.py for larger studies.
quality.noReport: No persisted report yet. Configure a small grid above and hit Run, or generate one offline with scripts/evaluate_baseline_vs_ai.py.
quality.legend.dz: Cohen's d_z — standardized matched-pair effect size
quality.legend.direction: shown only when p < α (significant difference)
quality.toastTooLarge: Grid is too large ({{n}} pairs). Reduce to ≤ 12.
quality.toastEmpty: Pick at least one planet, one style and one seed.

explain.title: Why does this piece sound like this?
explain.whyTitle: Why {{planet}} sounds the way it does

glossary.title: Glossary of terms
glossary.subtitle: A focused dictionary of the astronomy, physics, music and AI vocabulary you will encounter in this product. Click any term in the app to jump straight here.

lab.title: Design your own orbit
lab.subtitle: Move the two sliders. Watch the orbit deform and Kepler's third law update the orbital period in real time. Hit Sonify to hear the music your custom object would make if it were a real planet.
lab.name: Object name
lab.nameHint: Letters, numbers and a few punctuation characters only (max 40 chars).
lab.namePlaceholder: Halley's Comet, Asteroid Bennu, Planet X…
lab.semiMajorHint: The average distance from the Sun. Drives the orbital period via Kepler's third law.
lab.previewHint: Updated automatically when you move the sliders. The yellow dot is the Sun; the larger yellow point on the orbit marks perihelion (closest pass).

kepler.title: Hear the three laws of planetary motion
kepler.subtitle: A guided walkthrough. Each tab pairs a slider, a visualization and a one-line statement of one of Kepler's three laws.
kepler.law1.title: First law: orbits are ellipses
kepler.law1.lead: Every planet's orbit is an ellipse with the Sun at one focus — not a perfect circle.
kepler.law1.body1: The shape is described by the
kepler.law1.body2: e: 0 is a circle, 1 is an escape trajectory. Drag the slider to deform the orbit while keeping the semi-major axis fixed at 1 AU.
kepler.law1.bullet3: The Sun always sits at one focus — never at the geometric center (unless e = 0).
kepler.law1.caption: Yellow dot = the Sun at one focus. The dashed circle is the orbit you would have with the same a but e = 0.
kepler.law2.title: Second law: equal areas in equal times
kepler.law2.lead: A line from the planet to the Sun sweeps out equal areas during equal time intervals.
kepler.law2.body1: That means the planet moves faster near
kepler.law2.body3: — exactly the asymmetry you hear in the comet-like presets of the orbital lab.
kepler.law2.ratio: Max speed is {{ratio}}× the min speed for this orbit.
kepler.law2.caption: Each colored wedge covers the same amount of time. Wedges near the Sun are 'fatter' (the body moves further per unit time).
kepler.law3.title: Third law: period vs distance
kepler.law3.lead: T² (period in years) divided by a³ (semi-major axis in AU) is the same constant for every body orbiting the Sun.
kepler.law3.body: Slide the two semi-major axes below. Watch the ratio in the rightmost column stay essentially constant — that is the
kepler.law3.takeaway: If a doubles, T grows by 2^1.5 ≈ 2.83 — so Mars's year (a ≈ 1.5) is almost 1.9 Earth years.
kepler.law3.caption: Cyan = Object A. Pink = Object B. Both orbits are nearly circular for clarity.

missions.title: 60+ years of getting off this rock
missions.subtitle: A curated timeline of the most consequential space missions, from Sputnik to JWST. Filter by category, search by mission name or agency, then jump straight into the planet's encyclopedia page.
missions.openArtemis: Replay Artemis I in the Studio

compare.title: Compare two planets, hear the difference
compare.subtitle: Pick any two planets, share a seed and a style, then read three layers of differences side-by-side: audio, physical properties from NASA, and sonification metrics. The single best classroom demo.
compare.physicsHint: These are the constants the sonification engine reads — orbital period, eccentricity, gravity — for each planet.
compare.metricsHint: Computed on the generated MIDI events. Larger absolute Δ% means the music is more different between the two planets.

encyclopedia.footer: Content derived from NASA / JPL public-domain fact sheets. Sonification copy by the AI Star Composer team.
encyclopedia.heroSubtitle: Curated NASA-sourced data, mission history, and the musical signature of each planet — written for classrooms, museums and curious minds.
encyclopedia.exportPdfHint: Generate a printable classroom worksheet for this planet (no upload, fully local).
encyclopedia.noImagery: No imagery curated for this planet yet.
encyclopedia.noMissions: No flagship spacecraft has visited this world yet.
encyclopedia.field.periodHint: Time to complete one orbit around the Sun
encyclopedia.field.tiltHint: Angle of the rotation axis vs the orbital plane

demo.title: Demo mode
demo.subtitle: A guided 3-planet tour for the jury. Press Start, sit back, listen.
demo.controlsHint: Earth · Mars · Jupiter — fixed seed 42 for reproducibility, 30-day Horizons window each.
demo.emptyState: Press Start to generate the demo set.

achievements.title: Discovery badges
achievements.empty: Generate, explore the encyclopedia, and run a lab to start unlocking badges.

birthday.title: Give a song your planet has been singing since you were born
birthday.subtitle: Pick a planet and a birth date — we pull real NASA Horizons ephemeris from that day to today, and turn it into a personalized symphony.

form.messagePlaceholder: Happy birthday — listen to how your planet has been singing since you arrived.
form.privacyNote: Gifts are stored on this server and reachable through a 24-character token. No account, no email — share the link only with the recipient.

celebration.label: Celebration
celebration.description: Bright, festive, danceable. Major hexatonic with a steady backbeat — the default party feel.

tender.label: Tender dedication
tender.description: Slow, intimate, warm. Major-7 piano over a warm pad — for close family, partners, or anyone who deserves a hug in audio form.

anthem.label: Cosmic anthem
anthem.description: Heroic, soaring, life-montage. Lydian #4 over horns, cello, and strings — for milestone birthdays.

waltz.label: Starlit waltz
waltz.description: Elegant, ballroom-flavored. Harpsichord and pizzicato over G major — feels like a classical dedication.

nebula.label: Nebula drift
nebula.description: Ethereal, ambient, cosmic. Glockenspiel over bowed pads, open fifth-stacked voicings.

placeholder.title: Your cosmic card will appear here
placeholder.body: Fill in a name, a birth date, and pick a planet. The card will include cosmic facts and an audio player you can download or share.

card.title: Happy birthday, {{name}} — your {{planet}} song
card.subtitle: Built from real NASA Horizons data, from {{date}} until today, for {{planet}}.
card.audioUnavailable: Audio is not ready yet — try regenerating in a moment.
card.fluidFallback: Playing the fallback synth — install a SoundFont (assets/soundfonts/) for studio-grade audio.

toasts.shareFailed: Clipboard blocked — copy the URL from the address bar.

gift.title: Open your cosmic birthday gift
gift.errorInvalid: This link doesn’t look like a valid gift token.

firstLaunch.desc: Open the app for the first time.

planetVs.desc: Compare two planets in the encyclopedia.
